title: Translation Validation Pipeline
description: Checks translation file registrations, key validity, and i18n() usage across the codebase for missing or invalid entries.
global_state: hadError: boolean (initially false) — set to true by reportError(); controls exit code

activities.name: checkTranslationRegistrations()
steps: Read all files from './src/gui/src/i18n/translations' via fs.promises.readdir
For each file: skip non-.js files
BRANCH: if not found → reportError('not listed in translations.js') → continue
BRANCH: if translation.name missing → reportError('missing a name')
BRANCH: if translation.code missing → reportError('missing a code')
BRANCH: if translation.code !== translationName → reportError('code mismatch')
BRANCH: if typeof translation.dictionary !== 'object' → reportError('missing translations dictionary')

activities.name: checkTranslationKeys()
steps: BRANCH: if key not in enDictionary → reportError('key does not exist in en')

activities.name: checkTranslationUsage()
steps: Read all files recursively via fs.promises.readdir(dir, { recursive: true })
For each file: skip non-.js files
Apply matchAll(i18nRegex) to find all i18n() calls
For each match: extract key = use[2]
BRANCH: if key not in enDictionary → reportError('Unrecognized i18n key: ...')

branches.action: Write 'Errors were found...' to stdout → process.exit(1)
Write '✅ Translations appear valid.' to stdout → process.exit(0)

key_conditions: translation not registered → reportError, continue loop
dictionary key missing from en → reportError
i18n() key not in enDictionary → reportError
hadError after all checks → exit(1), else exit(0)
important_notes: Pipeline runs three sequential validation phases. All phases run regardless of earlier errors (fail-accumulate, not fail-fast). hadError is a global flag. Async phases use fs.promises. i18n() regex uses capture group 2 for the key. Only en.js is the reference/ground-truth dictionary.
